heading: Enabling middle mile broadband infrastructure
label: 1741 Enabling middle mile broadband infrastructure
notes: References in TextThis Act, referred to in subsec. (a)(16)(B)(iii)(II), (17)(B)(ii)(II), means div. F of , , , see Definitions note set out below. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 117–58135 Stat. 1182Nov. 15, 2021

children.heading: Anchor institution
children.label: 1 Anchor institution
children.text: The term “anchor institution” means a school, library, medical or healthcare provider, community college or other institution of higher education, or other community support organization or entity.

children.heading: Assistant Secretary
children.label: 2 Assistant Secretary
children.text: The term “Assistant Secretary” means the Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Communications and Information.

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 3 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Federal Communications Commission.

children.label: A
children.text: a State, political subdivision of a State, Tribal government, technology company, electric utility, utility cooperative, public utility district, telecommunications company, telecommunications cooperative, nonprofit foundation, nonprofit corporation, nonprofit institution, nonprofit association, regional planning counsel, Native entity, or economic development authority; or

children.label: B
children.text: a partnership of 2 or more entities described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: FCC fixed broadband map
children.label: 5 FCC fixed broadband map
children.text: The term “FCC fixed broadband map” means the map created by the Commission under .section 642(c)(1)(B) of this title

children.heading: Indian Tribe
children.label: 6 Indian Tribe
children.text: The term “Indian Tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Interconnect
children.label: 7 Interconnect
children.text: The term “interconnect” means the physical linking of 2 networks for the mutual exchange of traffic on non-discriminatory terms and conditions.

children.heading: Internet exchange facility
children.label: 8 Internet exchange facility
children.text: The term “internet exchange facility” means physical infrastructure through which internet service providers and content delivery networks exchange internet traffic between their networks.

children.label: A
children.text: means any broadband infrastructure that does not connect directly to an end-user location, including an anchor institution; and

children.label: i
children.text: leased dark fiber, interoffice transport, backhaul, carrier-neutral internet exchange facilities, carrier-neutral submarine cable landing stations, undersea cables, transport connectivity to data centers, special access transport, and other similar services; and

children.label: ii
children.text: wired or private wireless broadband infrastructure, including microwave capacity, radio tower access, and other services or infrastructure for a private wireless broadband network, such as towers, fiber, and microwave links.

children.heading: Middle mile grant
children.label: 10 Middle mile grant
children.text: The term “middle mile grant” means a grant awarded under subsection (c).

children.label: A
children.text: an Indian Tribe;

children.label: B
children.text: an Alaska Native Corporation;

children.label: C
children.text: a Native Hawaiian organization (as defined in ;section 7517 of title 20

children.label: D
children.text: the Department of Hawaiian Home Lands; and

children.label: E
children.text: the Office of Hawaiian Affairs.

children.heading: State
children.label: 12 State
children.text: The term “State” has the meaning given the term in .section 153 of this title

children.heading: Submarine cable landing station
children.label: 13 Submarine cable landing station
children.text: The term “submarine cable landing station” means a cable landing station, as that term is used in section 1.767(a)(5) of title 47, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation), that can be utilized to land a submarine cable by an entity that has obtained a license under the first section of the Act entitled “An Act relating to the landing and operation of submarine cables in the United States”, approved () (commonly known as the “Cable Landing Licensing Act”).May 27, 192147 U.S.C. 34

children.heading: Tribal government
children.label: 14 Tribal government
children.text: The term “Tribal government” means the recognized governing body of any Indian or Alaska Native tribe, band, nation, pueblo, village, community, component band, or component reservation, individually identified (including parenthetically) in the list published most recently as of , pursuant to .November 15, 2021section 5131 of title 25

children.heading: Trust land
children.label: 15 Trust land
children.text: The term “trust land” has the meaning given the term in .section 3765 of title 38

children.label: A
children.text: that is designated as a Tribally underserved area through the process described in subsection (g); or

children.label: i
children.text: is of a standard size not larger than a census block, as established by the Commission;

children.label: ii
children.text: is not an unserved area; and

children.label: aa
children.text: a download speed of not less than 100 megabits per second; and

children.label: bb
children.text: an upload speed of not less than 20 megabits per second; or

children.label: II
children.text: minimum download and upload speeds established as benchmarks by the Commission for purposes of this Act after , if those minimum speeds are higher than the minimum speeds required under subclause (I).November 15, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: that is designated as a Tribally underserved area through the process described in subsection (g); or

children.label: i
children.text: is of a standard size not larger than a census block, as established by the Commission; and

children.label: aa
children.text: a download speed of not less than 25 megabits per second; and

children.label: bb
children.text: an upload speed of not less than 3 megabits per second; or

children.label: II
children.text: minimum download and upload speeds established as benchmarks by the Commission for purposes of this Act after , if those minimum speeds are higher than the minimum speeds required under subclause (I).November 15, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: to encourage the expansion and extension of middle mile infrastructure to reduce the cost of connecting unserved and underserved areas to the backbone of the internet (commonly referred to as the “last mile”); and

children.label: B
children.text: to promote broadband connection resiliency through the creation of alternative network connection paths that can be designed to prevent single points of failure on a broadband network.

children.label: i
children.text: projects that leverage existing rights-of-way, assets, and infrastructure to minimize financial, regulatory, and permitting challenges;

children.label: ii
children.text: projects in which the eligible entity designs the route of the middle mile infrastructure to enable the connection of unserved anchor institutions, including Tribal anchor institutions; and

children.label: iii
children.text: projects that facilitate the development of carrier-neutral interconnection facilities; and

children.label: I
children.text: improve the redundancy and resiliency of existing middle mile infrastructure; and

children.label: II
children.text: reduce regulatory and permitting barriers to promote the construction of new middle mile infrastructure; and

children.label: i
children.text: facilitate increased broadband resiliency or redundancy of existing middle mile infrastructure; or

children.label: ii
children.text: provide connectivity to unserved areas and underserved areas within the service territory of the utility and nearby communities.

children.heading: Middle mile grants
children.label: c Middle mile grants
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall establish a program under which the Assistant Secretary makes grants on a technology-neutral, competitive basis to eligible entities for the construction, improvement, or acquisition of middle mile infrastructure.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall establish an application process for middle mile grants in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: A
children.text: The eligible entity adopts fiscally sustainable middle mile strategies.

children.label: B
children.text: The eligible entity commits to offering non-discriminatory interconnect to terrestrial and wireless last mile broadband providers and any other party making a bona fide request.

children.label: i
children.text: expressed written interest in interconnecting with middle mile infrastructure planned to be deployed by the eligible entity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: demonstrated sustainable business plans or adequate funding sources with respect to the interconnect described in clause (i).

children.label: D
children.text: The eligible entity has identified supplemental investments or in-kind support (such as waived franchise or permitting fees) that will accelerate the completion of the planned project.

children.label: E
children.text: The eligible entity has demonstrated that the middle mile infrastructure will benefit national security interests of the United States and the Department of Defense.

children.heading: Grant application competence
children.label: 3 Grant application competence
children.text: The Assistant Secretary shall include in the application process established under paragraph (1) a requirement that an eligible entity provide evidence that the eligible entity is capable of carrying out a proposed project in a competent manner, including by demonstrating that the eligible entity has the financial, technical, and operational capability to carry out the proposed project and operate the resulting middle mile broadband network.

children.label: A
children.text: connecting middle mile infrastructure to last mile networks that provide or plan to provide broadband service to households in unserved areas;

children.label: B
children.text: connecting non-contiguous trust lands; or

children.label: C
children.text: the offering of wholesale broadband service at reasonable rates on a carrier-neutral basis.

children.heading: Buildout timeline
children.label: 2 Buildout timeline
children.text: Subject to paragraph (5), to be eligible to obtain a middle mile grant, an eligible entity shall agree, in the application submitted through the process established under subsection (d), to complete buildout of the middle mile infrastructure described in the application by not later than 5 years after the date on which amounts from the grant are made available to the eligible entity.

children.heading: Capability to support retail broadband service
children.label: A Capability to support retail broadband service
children.text: A project shall be eligible for a middle mile grant if, at the time of the application, the Assistant Secretary determines that the proposed middle mile broadband network will be capable of supporting retail broadband service.

children.heading: Use of most recent data
children.label: i Use of most recent data
I
children.text: The FCC fixed broadband map.

children.label: II
children.text: The State in which the area that will be served by the middle mile infrastructure is located, or the Tribal government with jurisdiction over the area that will be served by the middle mile infrastructure (if applicable).

children.label: aa
children.text: demonstrate that more than 25 percent of the respondents display a broadband service speed that is slower than the speeds required for an area to qualify as unserved; and

children.label: AA
children.text: the eligible entity;

children.label: BB
children.text: the State in which the area that will be served by the middle mile infrastructure is located; or

children.label: CC
children.text: the Tribal government with jurisdiction over the area that will be served by the middle mile infrastructure (if applicable).

children.label: aa
children.text: the Assistant Secretary;

children.label: bb
children.text: the Commission;

children.label: cc
children.text: the Tribal government with jurisdiction over the area that will be served by the middle mile infrastructure (if applicable); and

children.label: dd
children.text: the State broadband office for the State in which the area that will be served by the middle mile infrastructure is located.

children.heading: Provision of information
children.label: II Provision of information
children.text: Subject to subclauses (III) and (IV), an eligible entity that constructs, improves, or acquires middle mile infrastructure using a middle mile grant shall share with each covered recipient the location of all the middle mile broadband infrastructure.

children.heading: Format
children.label: III Format
children.text: An eligible entity shall provide the information required under subclause (II) to each covered recipient in a uniform format determined by the Assistant Secretary.

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: The information provided by an eligible entity under subclause (II) may only be used for purposes of carrying out the grant program under subsection (c) and any reporting related thereto.

children.heading: In general
children.label: AA In general
children.text: A covered recipient may not receive information under subclause (II) unless the covered recipient agrees in writing to assert all available legal defenses to the disclosure of the information if a person or entity seeks disclosure from the covered recipient under any Federal, State, or local public disclosure law.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: BB Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in subitem (AA) is intended to be or shall be construed as a waiver of Tribal sovereign immunity.

children.label: I
children.text: 1 gigabit per second for downloads; and

children.label: II
children.text: 1 gigabit per second for uploads to an anchor institution; and

children.label: ii
children.text: include direct interconnect facilities that will facilitate the provision of broadband service to anchor institutions located within 1,000 feet of the middle mile infrastructure.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An eligible entity that receives a middle mile grant to build a middle mile project using fiber optic technology shall offer interconnection in perpetuity, where technically feasible without exceeding current or reasonably anticipated capacity limitations, on reasonable rates and terms to be negotiated with requesting parties.

children.heading: Nature of interconnection
children.label: ii Nature of interconnection
children.text: The interconnection required to be offered under clause (i) includes both the ability to connect to the public internet and physical interconnection for the exchange of traffic.

children.heading: Inclusion in application
children.label: iii Inclusion in application
children.text: An applicant for a middle mile grant shall disclose the applicant’s proposed interconnection, nondiscrimination, and network management practices in the application submitted through the process established under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: establish sufficient transparency, accountability, reporting, and oversight measures for the grant program established under subsection (c) to deter waste, fraud, and abuse of program funds; and

children.label: i
children.text: buildout requirements for each eligible entity that receives a middle mile grant, which shall require the completion of a certain percentage of project miles by a certain date; and

children.label: ii
children.text: penalties, which may include rescission of funds, for grantees that do not meet requirements described in clause (i) or the deadline under paragraph (2).

children.label: i
children.text: the eligible entity has a plan for use of the middle mile grant;

children.label: ii
children.text: the project to build out middle mile infrastructure is underway; or

children.label: iii
children.text: extenuating circumstances require an extension of time to allow completion of the project to build out middle mile infrastructure.

children.heading: Effect on interim buildout requirements
children.label: B Effect on interim buildout requirements
children.text: If the Assistant Secretary grants an extension under subparagraph (A), the Assistant Secretary shall modify any buildout requirements established under paragraph (4)(B)(i) as necessary.

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: f Federal share
children.text: The amount of a middle mile grant awarded to an eligible entity may not exceed 70 percent of the total project cost.

children.heading: Special rules for Tribal governments
children.label: g Special rules for Tribal governments
A
children.text: for the effective delivery and administration of middle mile grants to Tribal governments; or

children.label: B
children.text: the construction, improvement, or acquisition of middle mile infrastructure on trust land.

children.heading: Tribally unserved areas; tribally underserved areas
children.label: 2 Tribally unserved areas; tribally underserved areas
children.text: The Assistant Secretary, in consultation with Tribal governments and Native entities, shall develop a process for designating Tribally unserved areas and Tribally underserved areas for purposes of this section.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $1,000,000,000 for fiscal years 2022 through 2026.
